seoTitle: Integrate Outlook Emails with Monday.com (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Turn incoming Outlook emails into actionable Monday.com tasks automatically and send outbound emails directly from your board.
intro: Jumping between your inbox and your project board to log every client email wastes time and creates gaps. This guide covers connecting Microsoft Outlook to Monday.com so incoming emails automatically become board items, with sender, subject, and body mapped into columns, plus outbound automations for replying straight from a status change.

steps.heading: Open the Integration Center
steps.body: From your master board, access the Integration Center and locate the official Microsoft Outlook card.

steps.heading: Choose an automated email recipe
steps.body: Set up a workflow recipe, such as creating a new item in a specific group whenever a new email arrives in your inbox.

steps.heading: Authenticate your Microsoft account
steps.body: Sign in with your Microsoft 365 or Outlook account and grant the required application permissions.

steps.heading: Map email data to board columns
steps.body: Match dynamic tokens like Subject, Body, Sender, and Date Received to your custom board columns.

steps.heading: Filter inbound emails
steps.body: Set conditional triggers to filter email ingestion by specific subject keywords, folders, or senders.

steps.heading: Set up outbound automation
steps.body: Configure automations to reply to emails or send notifications automatically when a status changes on your board.

faq.question: Can I filter which emails create board items?
faq.answer: Yes, you can configure conditional triggers to filter inbound email ingestion by subject keywords, specific folders, or senders.

faq.question: Can I reply to a client's email from Monday.com?
faq.answer: Yes, outbound automation can be set up to send emails or notifications automatically when a status changes on your board.

faq.question: What email data gets pulled into my board?
faq.answer: Dynamic tokens like Subject, Body, Sender, and Date Received map directly into your custom board columns.

faq.question: Do I need Microsoft 365 for this to work?
faq.answer: Yes, you authenticate with a Microsoft 365 or Outlook account to grant the integration access.
